title: Values
description: Vocabulary describing personal values and character.

entries.definition: Kind and willing to give or share.
entries.example: She was very generous with her time.

entries.definition: Always telling the truth.
entries.example: People trust him because he is honest.

entries.definition: Able to stay calm while waiting or dealing with difficulties.
entries.example: Good teachers are patient with their students.

entries.definition: Showing courage in difficult or dangerous situations.
entries.example: The firefighter was brave during the rescue.

entries.definition: Treating everyone equally and in a reasonable way.
entries.example: A good teacher should be fair to every student.

entries.definition: Friendly, caring and helpful towards other people.
entries.example: She was very kind to the new student.

entries.definition: Showing politeness and consideration for other people.
entries.example: The children were respectful during the visit.

entries.definition: Reliable and able to make good decisions.
entries.example: He is responsible for looking after the class keys.

entries.definition: Caring only about yourself and not about other people.
entries.example: It was selfish to eat all the cake.

entries.definition: Wanting more money, food or possessions than you need.
entries.example: The greedy character wanted all the treasure.

entries.definition: Always supporting a person, group or belief.
entries.example: She remained loyal to her friends.

entries.definition: Able to be trusted to do what is expected.
entries.example: He is a reliable colleague who always arrives on time.

entries.definition: Sure of yourself and your abilities.
entries.example: A confident speaker can still make mistakes.

entries.definition: Able to do things without needing help from others.
entries.example: Adult learners often become more independent over time.

entries.definition: Having a strong wish to succeed or achieve something.
entries.example: She is ambitious and wants to improve her English quickly.

entries.definition: Not willing to give up.
entries.example: He was determined to pass his exam.

entries.definition: Thinking about other people's needs and feelings.
entries.example: It was thoughtful of her to bring extra copies.

entries.definition: Not polite or respectful.
entries.example: It is rude to interrupt someone while they are speaking.

entries.definition: Showing good manners and respect for others.
entries.example: British people often use polite phrases like please and thank you.

entries.definition: Able to change plans or ideas when needed.
entries.example: A flexible learner can try different study methods.
